$comment: Tier-2 DB-state fixture spec for the `wardrobe` repo (Phase-2) over the
`wardrobe_items` table. One committed file drives BOTH sides of the
differential:
- the v4 oracle (build-wardrobe-fixture.ts + wardrobe-tier2.ts) and
- the Rust port (quilltap-harness wardrobe tier-2 test).
WHY BASE SQL CRUD, NOT THE PUBLIC OVERRIDES: v4's WardrobeRepository overrides
create/update/delete to be VAULT-ONLY (they write to the character document
store and throw without a mount — there is no SQL write mirror). So they can't
run against a bare fixture DB. This differential instead exercises v4's REAL
base-repository SQL CRUD (`_create`/`_update`/`_delete`) against the
`wardrobe_items` table — the column set/affinity the schema-translator builds
from WardrobeItemSchema and the row marshaling that backs the table's reads
(findByCharacterIdRaw -> findByFilter). Both the fixture builder and the oracle
case drive a thin test subclass that exposes those protected base internals.
What this repo banks: TWO JSON ARRAY columns (`types` — enum strings; and
`componentItemIds`), TWO boolean columns (`isDefault`, `replace` -> INTEGER
0/1), a nullable soft-delete TIMESTAMP column (`archivedAt`, 'date' affinity ->
TEXT, exercised null and non-null), and several nullable string/UUID columns
(`characterId` null-for-archetype, `description`, `imagePrompt`,
`appropriateness`, `migratedFromClothingRecordId`). Scope: create / update /
delete only.
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key, the same one the other
fixtures use. It is NOT the real Friday pepper and is safe to commit.
DETERMINISM: every id and timestamp is pinned, so both sides produce
byte-identical post-op state and the canonical dump needs ZERO normalization.

seed.title: Navy Peacoat
seed.imagePrompt: double-breasted navy wool peacoat with brass buttons

ops.comment: New leaf item on a character. Exercises a single-type array, an EMPTY componentItemIds array, both bools (false), and several null columns; id + timestamps pinned.
New composite archetype (characterId null). Multi-type array, non-empty componentItemIds, replace=true — the full-outfit-swap shape.
Update seed item #1: revise description + REPLACE the types array (top -> top,accessories) + flip isDefault off. updatedAt pinned, createdAt preserved.

data.description: A heavy double-breasted wool coat, now with a scarf.

ops.comment: SOFT-DELETE (archive) seed item #2: set archivedAt to a timestamp (the `archive` shape). Exercises the nullable-timestamp column being SET to non-null on update.
